title: dicliptera babui
intro: Dicliptera babui, also known as Red Beggartick or Red Beggartickle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Dicliptera babui include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
